description: Scaffold 4 new benchmark suites, create config scripts, update manifest/status scripts, archive saturated ccb_repoqa, and create shared scorer templates for the gap-fill expansion.

userStories.title: Scaffold 4 new benchmark suite directories
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want the directory structure for ccb_nlqa, ccb_security, ccb_onboarding, and ccb_docgen created with suite-level CLAUDE.md and shared test harness templates.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_nlqa/ directory exists with CLAUDE.md describing NL codebase Q&A tasks
benchmarks/ccb_security/ directory exists with CLAUDE.md describing security vulnerability triage tasks
benchmarks/ccb_onboarding/ directory exists with CLAUDE.md describing onboarding exploration tasks
benchmarks/ccb_docgen/ directory exists with CLAUDE.md describing doc generation tasks
Each CLAUDE.md includes search strategy guidance for Sourcegraph MCP tools relevant to that task type
Each CLAUDE.md specifies the output format and path (e.g., /logs/agent/investigation.md, /workspace/documentation.md, /logs/agent/triage.md, /logs/agent/onboarding.md)
A shared weighted-checklist test.sh template exists at benchmarks/templates/weighted_checklist_scorer.sh (generalized from ccb_investigation's test.sh)
userStories.notes: Model after ccb_investigation's CLAUDE.md pattern. The shared scorer template should be parameterizable — each task copies it and just needs its own ground_truth.json.

userStories.title: Create 4 new _2config.sh run scripts
userStories.description: As a benchmark operator, I want config scripts for the 4 new suites so I can launch baseline + SG_full runs.
acceptanceCriteria: configs/nlqa_2config.sh exists and follows the pattern from configs/investigation_2config.sh
configs/security_2config.sh exists
configs/onboarding_2config.sh exists
configs/docgen_2config.sh exists
Each script sources configs/_common.sh and supports --parallel, --baseline-only, --full-only, --task flags
Each script has an empty TASKS=() array with a comment 'populated by task-creation Ralphs'
Each script uses enforce_subscription_mode and setup_dual_accounts
bash -n configs/nlqa_2config.sh passes (syntax valid for all 4 scripts)
userStories.notes: Copy investigation_2config.sh as the template. Change SUITE_NAME, RUN_PREFIX, and BENCHMARK_DIR per suite. Leave TASKS array empty — task-creation Ralphs will populate it.

userStories.title: Update generate_manifest.py and aggregate_status.py for new suites
userStories.description: As a benchmark operator, I want the MANIFEST generator and status aggregator to recognize the 4 new suite prefixes.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/generate_manifest.py DIR_PREFIX_TO_SUITE dict includes: 'nlqa_' -> 'ccb_nlqa', 'security_' -> 'ccb_security', 'onboarding_' -> 'ccb_onboarding', 'docgen_' -> 'ccb_docgen'
scripts/aggregate_status.py DIR_PREFIX_TO_SUITE dict includes the same 4 mappings
python3 scripts/generate_manifest.py runs without errors
python3 scripts/aggregate_status.py --help runs without errors
userStories.notes: Look for the existing DIR_PREFIX_TO_SUITE dictionary in both scripts and add the new entries alongside existing ones.

userStories.title: Archive saturated ccb_repoqa benchmark
userStories.description: As a benchmark maintainer, I want to formally archive ccb_repoqa since it scores 1.000/1.000 on both configs and provides zero signal.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_repoqa/ directory has been moved to benchmarks/archive/ccb_repoqa/
All repoqa entries removed from configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json
configs/repoqa_2config.sh moved to configs/archive/repoqa_2config.sh (or deleted with comment in commit)
git log shows a commit with message explaining the archival reason (ceiling saturation)
python3 scripts/generate_manifest.py still runs without errors after archival
userStories.notes: Check if there's a configs/repoqa_2config.sh or repoqa_3config.sh. Move it too. Don't delete — archive for reference.

userStories.title: Create F1 scorer template for structured JSON output
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a reusable F1 scorer for tasks that output structured JSON (callers.json, implementors.json, etc.) so cross-repo symbol lookup tasks can score precision and recall.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/templates/f1_json_scorer.sh exists
Scorer reads agent output JSON from a configurable path (default: /workspace/output.json)
Scorer reads ground truth JSON from /tests/ground_truth.json
Computes precision, recall, and F1 score based on matching entries by a configurable key field
Writes final F1 score (0.0-1.0) to /logs/verifier/reward.txt
Handles edge cases: empty output (score=0), no ground truth (error), malformed JSON (score=0)
bash -n benchmarks/templates/f1_json_scorer.sh passes (syntax valid)
userStories.notes: The scorer should match entries by a composite key (e.g., repo+file+function) and compute standard IR metrics. Look at ccb_codereview's test.sh for F1 scoring pattern to reuse.
